title: Global markets fall due to tech weakness and oil price rise
summary: On Friday, July 17, global stock indices recorded losses, with the Nasdaq leading the declines due to a massive sell-off in tech and chip stocks, while oil soared amid tensions in the Middle East.

title: SpaceX shares plummet after failed Starship launch
summary: The aborted test of the Starship rocket causes a sharp stock market drop for the company, which had recently debuted at a record high.

title: Oil surges due to attacks between Iran and the US and fear of a Strait of Hormuz closure
summary: Crude prices rise sharply as Chevron explores an alternative route and oil companies increase production in Iraq to avoid a supply crisis.

title: Massive sell-off of semiconductor stocks drags down Asian and US markets
summary: Chip firms suffer their worst week in over a year due to fears of oversupply and falling demand for AI chips, affecting global stock markets.

title: Netflix disappoints with its forecasts despite beating profit expectations
summary: The streaming giant reported adjusted earnings slightly above expectations, but its revenue and future projections did not convince investors, causing a stock drop.

title: Apple surpasses Nvidia to become the world's most valuable company
summary: Apple's market capitalization reaches $4.9 trillion, displacing the chipmaker from the top spot amid fears of overinvestment in AI.

title: Warren Buffett will donate all his Berkshire Hathaway shares to foundations by 2034
summary: The billionaire investor has already delivered $6 billion in shares and plans to distribute the remaining $138 billion in the coming years, fulfilling his philanthropic promise.

title: Digi experiences a rollercoaster on its debut on the Spanish stock exchange
summary: The telecom operator rises 5% trying to recover from a turbulent start, amid doubts about its valuation and growth prospects.

title: EU delays methane fines and eases emissions rules to ease business burden
summary: Under pressure from the United States, Brussels postpones sanctions for non-compliance with methane regulations and relaxes emissions trading to prioritize competitiveness.

title: Intuitive Surgical plummets after reporting weak quarterly results
summary: Shares of the robotic surgery company fall more than 12% in aftermarket trading after its earnings failed to meet market expectations.

title: Options and ETF activity stands out on Wall Street with unusual volumes
summary: Options contracts on Western Union and other securities show great interest, along with ETFs like FFLG that register atypical movements in Friday's session.

title: Trump Media offers fast access to president's posts for $100,000 monthly
summary: Truth Social plans to sell priority access to Donald Trump's messages to banks and media, which has generated criticism and legal doubts.

title: Bestinver warns that many AI companies are overvalued
summary: The Spanish fund manager bets on artificial intelligence in the long term but warns of a bubble in the sector that could cause corrections.

title: Boeing to invest 160 billion yen to recover small aircraft production
summary: The company plans to return to production levels before the 737 MAX accident, opening a new assembly line.

title: Citi lowers Telefónica valuation and focuses on potential mergers
summary: The investment bank cuts its price target for the Spanish operator, noting that mergers and acquisitions will be key to its future.

title: Brussels proposes creating large-scale European banks to compete with the US
summary: The European Commission presents a plan to encourage cross-border mergers and reduce fragmentation in the European banking sector.

title: Eurozone inflation moderates slightly, but the ECB remains in a dilemma
summary: Inflation data shows a slight decrease, although price pressure remains high, complicating the European Central Bank's decision on rates.

title: PayPal board rejects acquisition offer as too low
summary: Executives of the payment company consider the offer insufficient, in a context of consolidation in the fintech sector.

title: Uber buys Delivery Hero for $14.8 billion in sector consolidation
summary: The acquisition could mark the end of the price war in food delivery, creating a global giant with dominant presence in Europe and Asia.

title: World Cup results influence stock performance of breweries
summary: Shares of brewing companies move based on the results of national teams in the World Cup, according to a sector analysis.
